summary: GM mode-flow implementation slice touching ten src/melee/gm files, with matching and cleanup work centered on gm_18A5.c, gm_1601.c, gm_1832.c, and gmmain_lib.c. Evidence shows code-shape cleanup, typed field recovery, local inline/helper experiments, and reviewer pressure to remove broad pragmas, raw extern float literals, and pointer math. Bot evidence reported 3 new matches and 40 unmatched-item improvements; the PR body said slice-specific CI/regression QA was still in progress, and a later author comment documents a narrow dont_inline guard around fn_80196CF8 needed to avoid a fn_8019B458 regression.

what_changed: PR body frames this as draft slice 1 of 14 from an aggregate WIP branch, scoped to src/melee/gm mode-flow implementation files.
gm_1601.c recovered fn_80160DE8 to a bot-reported 100% match and changed raw HSD_Text byte access at default_kerning to typed field access in the loaded diff.
gm_1601.c replaced generated-looking temp/goto-heavy color assignment logic in fn_801695BC with natural loops, local role names, and an explicit colors[12] buffer.
gm_1601.static.h split padding in lbl_8046B488_t and added an x1C0[0x1B] field, enabling gm_1601.c to use lbl_8046B488.x1C0 instead of base + 0x1C0 pointer arithmetic in fn_80169A84.
gm_1832.c introduced TrainingItemEntry with a size assert and inline accessors over lbl_803D9828 for item/text IDs.

gm_18A5.c contained multiple attempted matching tactics: temporary pointer variables for DrawRectangle color arguments, extern float labels for 0.0f/1.0f/4.5f, inline helpers around fn_80191240, pragmas/dont_inline guards, and byte pointer math in fn_801953C8.
Review comments requested resolving gm_18A5.c issues by inlining temporary vars, avoiding externs, keeping useful inlines, avoiding pragmas, and removing pointer math.

Author comment states removing the dont_inline guard around fn_80196CF8 caused a bot-reported nonmatched regression in fn_8019B458 from 78.78% to 77.90%; restoring only that narrow guard cleared the regression.

smart_moves.text: Added a real struct field for lbl_8046B488.x1C0 in gm_1601.static.h, then used that field in gm_1601.c instead of continuing raw base + offset access.
Replaced generated temp/goto style in fn_801695BC with readable loops and local role names while preserving the color shuffle/assignment structure shown in the diff.
Used a narrow codegen guard only after bot evidence showed removing it regressed a neighbor function, instead of retaining broader pragma cleanup unconditionally.
Introduced a typed TrainingItemEntry wrapper with STATIC_ASSERT for a raw table, localizing the cast behind accessors.

decomp_lessons.lesson: When a raw offset is stable enough to name, prefer adding the field to the local static struct and using that field at call sites rather than carrying base pointer arithmetic.
Generated-looking unrolled assignments and goto ladders can often be rewritten as natural for/while loops with small local role variables; fn_801695BC is direct PR evidence for this cleanup class.
Do not replace ordinary float literals with extern address-labeled symbols merely to chase codegen; review explicitly asked to resolve lbl_804DA6E8/lbl_804DA720 externs without using externs.

Pragmas and dont_inline guards need narrow evidence. This PR contains repeated review requests to remove pragmas, plus one explicit bot-regression note justifying a narrow dont_inline guard around fn_80196CF8.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text.md: fn_80196CF8 guard and fn_8019B458 regression

decomp_lessons.lesson: If a helper/inline boundary is needed for matching, reviewers may prefer preserving the inline form rather than flattening or replacing it with literal/global-symbol workarounds.
evidence_refs: review_comments.md: r3415823893 'resolving this keeping the inlines'

assembly_or_matching_tactics.details: The diff introduces inline helpers such as fn_80162170_inner, fn_80191240_inline*, TrainingItemTable_Get*, and gm_8016A22C_kind; review specifically asked to keep some inlines in gm_18A5.c.

Examples include score0-score3 temporaries in gm_80166A98, tmp_text/tmp_ckind in fn_80160DE8, and a local ptr to lbl_8046B668 in gm_8016AC44; PR evidence does not include per-tactic objdiff justification beyond bot aggregate improvements.
fn_80169A84 changed an if/else ladder into a switch over arg0 and used typed lbl_8046B488.x1C0 access.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Narrow dont_inline guard after regression evidence
assembly_or_matching_tactics.details: Author reports removing dont_inline around fn_80196CF8 regressed fn_8019B458 from 78.78% to 77.90%; restoring only this guard cleared the regression.
Review comments pushed back on temporary pointer variables for DrawRectangle color arguments, extern float symbols, pragmas/pool_data, and byte pointer math.

review_feedback.feedback: Inline temporary GXColor* variables introduced solely around DrawRectangle calls.
Resolve float constants without introducing extern f32 labels for 0.0f and 1.0f.
Keep useful inline boundaries when resolving fn_80191240 and nearby matching issues.
Resolve pragmas without using broad pragma push/pop or dont_inline blocks.
Remove or reduce byte pointer math in fn_801953C8, preferably using typed fields such as tm->x37[idx].x3/x5/x7.

Review excerpt mentions resolving '#pragma pool_data off' without pragmas, but gm_16F1.c is not listed among changed files in this loaded slice; treat as evidence inconsistency or possibly neighboring-file context.
evidence_refs: changed_files.jsonl: changed files do not include gm_16F1.c

The PR body says slice-specific CI/regression QA was in progress; bot output gives useful match/improvement evidence but the loaded record does not show full final validation details.
Several review comments are authored by fjooord, the PR author, so they are still PR evidence but should not be overread as independent reviewer consensus.
One review comment path is src/melee/gm/gm_16F1.c, which is not in changed_files.jsonl for this slice; retain as an evidence discrepancy rather than a confirmed changed file.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Recover local struct fields before retaining raw offset access
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2704 supports a reusable decomp lesson: when local struct ownership is clear, split padding and add a field such as lbl_8046B488_t.x1C0, then use field access instead of base + offset arithmetic.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Keep codegen tactics narrow and evidence-backed
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2704 supports a reusable tactic note: broad pragmas were reviewed against, but a narrow dont_inline guard around fn_80196CF8 had explicit bot-regression evidence involving fn_8019B458.

source_update_candidates.title: Add PR #2704 as an example for typed fields over pointer math
source_update_candidates.text: Possible example for typed-fields-over-pointer-math: gm_1601.static.h added lbl_8046B488_t.x1C0 and gm_1601.c used lbl_8046B488.x1C0 instead of base + 0x1C0; gm_18A5.c review separately asked whether byte pointer math in fn_801953C8 could be removed.
source_update_candidates.reason: This PR provides concrete examples matching the existing typed-fields-over-pointer-math standard.

source_update_candidates.title: Add PR #2704 as an example for avoiding extern float literal symbols
source_update_candidates.text: Possible example for literals/data ownership: review requested resolving extern f32 lbl_804DA6E8 // 0.0f and lbl_804DA720 // 1.0f without using externs.
source_update_candidates.reason: This directly illustrates the existing rule that ordinary literals should not become address-named data without ownership evidence.

source_update_candidates.title: Add PR #2704 as an example for narrow pragma/dont_inline exceptions
source_update_candidates.text: Possible example for matching-tactics-need-evidence and avoid-pragmas-register-asm: multiple review comments asked to remove pragmas, while a later author comment documents a narrow dont_inline guard retained only after bot evidence showed a fn_8019B458 regression.
source_update_candidates.reason: This PR cleanly contrasts broad rejected pragmas with a documented narrow exception.

source_update_candidates.title: GM mode-flow files often require local static struct/layout recovery
source_update_candidates.text: Potential path-scoped fact for src/melee/gm: in PR #2704, mode-flow matching work included local static struct refinement in gm_1601.static.h and typed wrappers/accessors for raw tables in gm_1832.c.
source_update_candidates.reason: Could help future GM decomp work, but needs curator comparison against existing path facts and current source.

rejection_notes: Do not promote a blanket rule that gm_18A5.c should always keep dont_inline around fn_80196CF8; PR evidence only supports that this slice observed one bot regression and used a narrow guard.
Do not treat all inline helper extraction in this PR as authored style; some helpers may be matching tactics, and loaded evidence does not show final objdiff for each.
Do not claim gm_16F1.c changed in PR #2704; the review excerpt references it, but changed_files.jsonl does not list it.
Do not promote final validation as fully clean from this slice alone; PR body says slice-specific QA was in progress, though merged metadata and bot report exist.
